D1 Optics L4 Analyze Reference

Spatial Coherence & Source Size

widen the source and watch a double slit's fringes lose their contrast

Light from different points of an extended source arrives at the two slits with random relative phase. The wider the source or the slits, the less correlated the light — and the weaker the fringes.

Set the source diameter, slit separation, and source distance. The strip shows fringe contrast; the curve plots visibility versus slit separation, with the first coherence null marked.
